rows.source_ref: Zilla.A, 'Bollinger Bands + Moving Average (BBMA Oma Ally) - Forex Trading Technique', Babypips forum thread, 2018, https://forums.babypips.com/t/bollinger-bands-moving-average-bbma-oma-ally-forex-trading-technique/136067
rows.strategy_name: BBMA Oma Ally (BB dynamic S/R candlestick method)
rows.concept: Bollinger Bands act as dynamic support/resistance so price or weighted MAs poking outside the band signals exhaustion and reverts inside, traded via discretionary Extrem/MHV/Re-entry candle setups.
rows.eligibility_reason: Discretionary candlestick method: no deterministic entry trigger, no SL/TP levels, timeframe unspecified ('follow TF'), entries keyed to subjective 'reversal candle'/'momentum'/'highest volume'; not mechanizable as stated.
rows.notes: Indicators are specified (BB 20/2/Close; LWMA5-High, LWMA10-High, LWMA5-Low, LWMA10-Low; EMA50-Close) but the three setups (Extrem, MHV loss-volume, Re-entry) are all one core discretionary framework, not distinct mechanizable strategies. Poster #10 argues the primary 'MA outside 2-SD BB' signal is near-degenerate (rarely if ever triggers). 'Highest/lowest volume' entry is ambiguous on FX (tick volume only). Only 1 of 5 MHV types disclosed. Multiple posters flag it as a vendor/MLM upsell for an 'advanced' paid course (reputability low). Generic BB mean-reversion kernel is implementable in principle but the source's actual system is underspecified; classified as one REJECTED summary row (zero mechanizable-as-stated candidates).

notes: Single discretionary BBMA framework; no distinct mechanizable candidates. Rules lack deterministic entry/exit and SL/TP.
Extracted text via pypdf (Read tool PDF rendering unavailable: pdftoppm/poppler not installed). Text layer was clean and complete across all 11 pages.
